LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO FORTY NINERS, LIMITED'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. A R G U E D. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE COURT DENIES SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. DEFENDANT'S NOTICE OF MOTION IS DEFECTIVE BECAUSE IT DOES NOT DESCRIBE THE CAUSES OF ACTION THAT ARE TO BE ADJUDICATED. DEFENDANT'S SEPARATE STATEMENT DOES NOT COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1350(D) OR (H) BECAUSE IT DOES NOT DESCRIBE THE CAUSES OF ACTION TO BE ADJUDICATED. IN ADDITION, DEFENDANT FAILED TO MAINTAIN ITS INITIAL SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN BECAUSE IT DID NOT ADDRESS ALL OF THE THEORIES OF LIABILITY ALLEGED IN THE AMENDED COMPLAINT. FOR EXAMPLE, DEFENDANT IGNORES PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGATION THAT DEFENDANT NEGLIGENTLY FAILED TO WARN PATRONS OF THE PRIOR SHOOTING. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

SUPPLEMENTAL POINTS & AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF MTN FOR RECONSIDERATION OF ORDER DENYING MTN TO SET ASIDE DISMISSAL FILED BY PLAINTIFF LONG, DANIEL

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF DANIEL LONG'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF ORDER DENYING MOTION TO SET ASIDE DISMISSAL IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT ADEQUATELY ALLEGE THAT NEW OR DIFFERENT FACTS, CIRCUMSTANCES, OR LAW EXIST WARRANTING RELIEF UNDER C.C.P. 1008. PLAINTIFF ARGUES THAT HE RELIED ON ADDISON V. STATE OF CALIFORNIA (1978) 21 CAL.3D 313 WHEN DISMISSING THIS ACTION AND THAT THIS COURT'S RULING ON ADDISON IN THE DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER [RELATED CASE #535439] IS A NEW FACT OR CIRCUMSTANCE OFFERING PLAINTIFF MANDATORY AND DISCRETIONARY RELIEF PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 473(B). HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE DISMISSAL DID NOT ASSERT RELIANCE ON ADDISON BUT INSTEAD ARGUED THAT HE DISMISSED THIS ACTION BASED ON THE BELIEF THAT DIVERSITY OF CITIZENSHIP EXISTED BETWEEN THE PARTIES. PLAINTIFF REFERRED TO ADDISON IN A FOOTNOTE IN HIS REPLY TO HIS MOTION TO SET ASIDE SUGGESTING THAT A C.C.P. 473(B) MOTION MAY BE APPROPRIATE DEPENDING ON THIS COURT'S RULING ON THE DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER IN RELATED CASE #535439, YET PLAINTIFF OFFERED NO ARGUMENT IN THAT MOTION OR ITS REPLY FOR HIS REASONABLE RELIANCE ON ADDISON IN DISMISSING HIS CASE. THIS COURT'S RULING ON ADDISON IN THE DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER IS NOT A NEW OR DIFFERENT FACT OR CIRCUMSTANCE THAT PRECLUDED THE PLAINTIFF FROM ARGUING HIS RELIANCE ON ADDISON WHEN DISMISSING THIS ACTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT THE NATIONAL FOOTBALL LEAGUE'S MOTION FOR ORDER PERMITTING CONFIDENTIAL MATERIALS TO BE FILED UNDER SEAL IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED AND THE MOTION COMPLIES WITH CRC 2.550 ET SEQ. THE COURT FINDS THAT THERE IS AN OVERRIDING INTEREST IN SEALING THE SECURITY DOCUMENTS THAT BENEFIT THE PUBLIC WELFARE, THAT INTEREST WILL BE PREJUDICED IF THE INFORMATION IS NOT SEALED, THE PROPOSED SEALING ORDER IS NARROWLY TAILORED, AND THERE IS NO LESS RESTRICTIVE MEANS TO ACHIEVE THE OVERRIDING INTEREST. THE COURT SIGNED THE REVISED PROPOSED ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

DISCOVERY 302, ANDREW MEEHAN'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART. SAN FRANCISCO 49ERS LTD. ("DEFENDANT") MUST PROVIDE A COMPLETE AND STRAIGHTFORWARD ANSWER TO INTERROGATORY NO. 12. THE RESPONSE WAS NOT OBJECTED TO ON RELEVANCE GROUNDS AND IS THUS NOT TIMELY RAISED. THE RESPONSE CAN BE QUALIFIED WITH AN EXPLANATION BUT MUST BE RESPONDED TO IN FULL AND THE IMPROPER OBJECTION THAT THE QUESTION "ASSUMES FACTS NOT IN EVIDENCE" SHOULD BE REMOVED. AS FOR INTERROGATORY NO. 13, DEFENDANT MUST INCLUDE A STATEMENT THAT "AFTER A REASONABLE AND GOOD FAITH EFFORT TO OBTAIN THE INFORMATION" IT DOES NOT HAVE DATA SUFFICIENT TO RESPOND TO THE INTERROGATORY." PLAINTIFF SHOULD STIPULATE THAT "WEAPON" MEANS "THINGS DESIGNED TO INFLICT BODILY HARM." INTERROGATORY NO. 13 SHOULD ALSO CONTAIN THE LANGUAGE THAT A REASONABLE AND GOOD FAITH EFFORT WAS MADE TO OBTAIN THE INFORMATION." THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO INTERROGATORY NO. 15. SANCTIONS ARE DENIED AS THE COURT FINDS SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION BY THE DEFENDANT IN RESPONDING AS IT DID. MOVING PARTY SHALL PROVIDE A PROPOSED ORDER TRACKING THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE PRO TEM: ARLENE BARTON; CLERK L. FONG; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Mtn For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Mtn To Set Aside Dismissal
Off Calendar Apr-16-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. KARA A. ABELSON OF THE LOMBARDI LOPER & CONANT, LLP, TEL. NO. (510) 433-2600 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF DANIEL LONG's Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Motion To Set Aside Dismissal is denied. Plaintiff does not adequately allege that new or different facts, circumstances, or law exist warranting relief under C.C.P. 1008. Plaintiff argues that he relied on Addison v. State of California (1978) 21 Cal.3d 313 when dismissing this action and that this Court's ruling on Addison in the Defendant's Demurrer [related case #535439] is a new fact or circumstance offering Plaintiff mandatory and discretionary relief pursuant to C.C.P. 473(b). However, Plaintiff's Motion to Set Aside the Dismissal did not assert reliance on Addison but instead argued that he dismissed this action based on the belief that diversity of citizenship existed between the parties. Plaintiff referred to Addison in a footnote in his Reply to his Motion to Set Aside suggesting that a C.C.P. 473(b) motion may be appropriate depending on this Court's ruling on the Defendant's Demurrer in related case #535439, yet Plaintiff offered no argument in that Motion or its Reply for his reasonable reliance on Addison in dismissing his case. This Court's ruling on Addison in the Defendant's Demurrer is not a new or different fact or circumstance that precluded the Plaintiff from arguing his reliance on Addison when dismissing this action. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/EHG

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-11-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: ATTY GEOFFREY BECKER, APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF DANIEL LONG;
MATTHEW S. CONANT, LOMBARDI LOPER & CONANT, 510.433.2600, APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO FORTY NINERS LTD.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO FORTY NINERS, LIMITED'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION: The Court denies summary judgment and summary adjudication. Defendant's notice of motion is defective because it does not describe the causes of action that are to be adjudicated. Defendant's separate statement does not comply with CRC 3.1350(d) or (h) because it does not describe the causes of action to be adjudicated. In addition, defendant failed to maintain its initial summary judgment burden because it did not address all of the theories of liability alleged in the amended complaint. For example, defendant ignores plaintiff's allegation that defendant negligently failed to warn patrons of the prior shooting. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185.

Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Responses To Interrogatories And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar May-23-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The tentative ruling is adopted as follows: ANDREW MEEHAN'S Motion To Compel Responses to Interrogatories and Request for Monetary Sanctions is Granted in part. San Francisco 49ers Ltd. ("defendant") must provide a complete and straightforward answer to Interrogatory No. 12. The response was not objected to on relevance grounds and is thus not timely raised. The response can be qualified with an explanation but must be responded to in full and the improper objection that the question "assumes facts not in evidence" should be removed. As for Interrogatory No. 13, defendant must include a statement that "after a reasonable and good faith effort to obtain the information" it does not have data sufficient to respond to the interrogatory." Plaintiff should stipulate that "weapon" means "Things designed to inflict bodily harm." Interrogatory No. 13 should also contain the language that a reasonable and good faith effort was made to obtain the information." The motion is denied as to Interrogatory No. 15. Sanctions are denied as the Court finds substantial justification by the Defendant in responding as it did. Moving party shall provide a proposed order tracking the tentative ruling. Judge Pro Tem: Arlene Barton; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)

Defendant San Francisco Forty Niners, Limited'S Motion For Protective Order Prohibiting The Depositions Of Dr. John York, Jed York And Trent Baalke
Off Calendar Apr-04-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: GEOFFREY BECKER, ESQ., 925.939.9041, FOR PLAINTIFF DANIEL LONG.
MATTHEW S. CONANT, ESQ., 510.433.2600, FOR DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO 49ERS LTD.

RULING: Argued and the Court modified its tentative ruling: Defendants' motion for protective order denied as to Jed York. Parties agree that the deposition of Jed York will last no more than one hour. Protective order granted as to the depositions of Trent Baalke and Dr. John York. Plaintiff will propound interrogatories to Mr. Baalke and Dr. John York within two weeks of hearing; Defendants agree to respond within 15 days thereafter. If, after receiving responses to interrogatories, Plaintiff determines that he still wishes to take the depositions of Mr. Baalke and/or Dr. York (and if Defendants will not agree), plaintiff may seek relief from this protective order. (counsel to meet and confer to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
